description: Underfoot, 'muila' covers ground, soil, and land in contrast with open water. Context can widen it to Earth as a whole, with 'ne muila' available when the planet is being named. Stone, sand, clay, and mud identify particular materials or conditions within the earth.
articulatory_notes: With the lips closed, voice 'm' through the nose into high-back rounded 'u'. Preserve the hiatus while moving the tongue forward and unrounding into stressed high-front 'i'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l', then lower the tongue into central 'a'.
usage_notes: The ecological expressions 'muila phialu' (groundwater, earth-water) and 'wirua muila menuro' (wetland, wet-earth habitat) keep their relevant relation visible. 'muroa' can describe fertile earth, while ecological condition, tenure, and access take their own words or clauses.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Soil condition affects food, water, habitat, and what can be built or restored there. Calling it earth keeps that shared ground in view without treating every use as harmless.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Cultivation, earthen building, pottery, and finding water depend on close knowledge of local soils. Texture, drainage, colour, and seasonal change can matter more than a general label.

semantic_domains.nature: soil, ground, dry land, and Earth as the terrestrial world
semantic_domains.physical: mineral and organic ground material underfoot
semantic_domains.spatial: land distinguished from open water in a place or route
